scenario: Junction Marketplace personalises a Camunda 8.8 Tasklist welcome form for 9200 customers per week, where the Text component must greet the user by name, list 3 to 5 hobby tags, and only render the whole block when a boolean variable usingTemplating is true. The developer is choosing the right syntax for the conditional, the variable interpolation, and the iteration inside the Text property.
question: Which template syntax does the Camunda 8.8 Text component require to express that personalised greeting?

options.text: A handlebars-style block wrapping the greeting with {{#if usingTemplating}} and {{/if}}, the user name interpolated as {{user.name}}, and the hobby tags iterated with {{#loop user.hobbies}} {{this}} {{/loop}}

An HTML-only template using <if cond='usingTemplating'> with nested <for each='user.hobbies'> elements, with custom HTML directives providing the templating rather than the Camunda templating language
A FEEL expression in the Text field combining if-then-else with for hobby in user.hobbies returning a Markdown string, with FEEL serving as the templating language for the Text component

A Jinja-style template with {% if usingTemplating %}, {{ user.name }}, and {% for hobby in user.hobbies %} loops, treating Jinja blocks as the documented templating engine for Camunda 8.8 Text components

a.text: Correct. The documented template syntax for the Text component uses {{#if usingTemplating}} ... {{/if}} for conditionals, {{user.name}} for variable expansion, and {{#loop user.hobbies}} ... {{this}} ... {{/loop}} for iteration over a list, exactly as shown in the example.

b.text: Incorrect. HTML directives such as <if> and <for> are not part of the Text component template syntax; the documented template uses handlebars-style double-brace markers, not custom HTML tags for control flow.

c.text: Incorrect. The Text component documents a handlebars-style templating syntax for the personalised content example, distinct from a FEEL expression with for/in comprehensions inside the Text field.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented templating syntax uses double-brace handlebars-style markers, not Jinja-style {% %} blocks; the example explicitly shows {{#if}}, {{#loop}}, {{this}}, and {{/loop}} forms.

explanation: The Text component supports a templating syntax using handlebars-style markers: {{#if cond}} ... {{/if}} for conditionals, {{path.to.variable}} for interpolation, and {{#loop list}} ... {{this}} ... {{/loop}} for iteration. The example in the docs uses precisely this syntax.
